mission.title: cilium: L7 visibility annotations do not work with proxylib parsers (including Kafka)
mission.description: L7 visibility annotations do not work with proxylib parsers (including Kafka). Requested by 17+ users.

steps.title: Check current cilium deployment
steps.description:
Verify your cilium version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cilium -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cilium
helm list -n cilium 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working cilium installation.

steps.title: Review cilium configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant cilium configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n cilium -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cilium
kubectl get configmap -n cilium -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=cilium
```
[Layer 7 Protocol Visibility](https://docs.cilium.io/en/stable/policy/visibility/) annotations only work for DNS and HTTP at the moment.

If you're interested in having this work for other L7 parsers, please :+1: or post below.

steps.title: Apply the fix for L7 visibility annotations do not work with proxylib parsers…
steps.description:
Translating VisibilityPolicy into PortNetworkPolicy to enable L7 visibility for pod annotations.

Creates an allow-all policy that has a PortNetworkPolicyRule derived from VisibilityPolicy.

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cilium -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cilium
kubectl get events -n cilium --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "L7 visibility annotations do not work with proxylib parsers…" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
Translating VisibilityPolicy into PortNetworkPolicy to enable L7 visibility for pod annotations.

Creates an allow-all policy that has a PortNetworkPolicyRule derived from VisibilityPolicy.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with cilium installed or the issue environment reproducible.
